name: Data Table
description: Data tables organize operational records such as invoices, clients, subscriptions, submissions, or campaign performance in a format that remains analytical yet visually aligned with the library's warm editorial character.
summary: Use the data table for dense comparison, sorting, and review workflows. The component should stay highly legible and structured, with restrained row treatments so numerical patterns remain easier to scan than decorative chrome.

variants.name: standard
variants.useCase: General multi-column reports with sorting and pagination.

variants.name: selectable
variants.useCase: Bulk workflows such as invoice approval, user management, or archive review.

variants.name: compact
variants.useCase: Dense dashboards where tables sit beside charts or narrative summaries.

contentGuidelines: Prefer clear, business-facing column labels such as Client, Amount, Renewal date, or Status.
Keep row actions consistent from page to page so operational teams build scanning memory.
Use muted supporting text for secondary metadata instead of adding extra columns too early.

layoutGuidelines.alignment: Right-align quantitative columns and left-align descriptive content for quicker financial or operational scanning.
layoutGuidelines.rowDensity: Maintain enough vertical space to preserve readability, especially because the typeface has strong personality.
layoutGuidelines.stickyHeader: Sticky headers are appropriate for long report views when the table remains the primary task surface.

accessibility.semantics: Use native table semantics whenever possible so column and row relationships stay explicit.
accessibility.sorting: Expose sort state through aria-sort on the active column header.
accessibility.selection: Bulk selection controls must announce counts and selected state clearly.

compositionNotes: Tables often live inside cards, but when the table becomes the main page artifact, it can sit directly on the page background with a warm bordered shell.
Accent-based hover states should be soft enough that row text remains easy to parse at speed.
Empty states should still feel editorial and composed, pairing one sentence of explanation with one recovery action.
